requirement_topic.en: Cybersecurity and Information Security — Mongolia Information and Cybersecurity Law 2021 and CITA / Ministry of Digital Development Oversight
requirement_topic.zh: 网络安全与信息安全——蒙古国2021年《信息与网络安全法》及CITA/数字发展与通信部监管
requirement_topic.zht: 網路安全與信息安全——蒙古國2021年《信息與網路安全法》及CITA/數字發展與通信部監管

summary.en: Mongolia enacted the Information and Cybersecurity Law in 2021, establishing a national framework for cybersecurity obligations applicable to information systems, critical information infrastructure, and operators of electronic communications services and connected devices. The law is administered jointly by CITA (Communications and Information Technology Authority) and the Ministry of Digital Development and Communications (formerly Ministry of Digital Development, Innovation and Aerospace Industry). Key obligations under the 2021 law include: notification requirements for cybersecurity incidents affecting information systems; security requirements for operators of critical information infrastructure; and baseline cybersecurity standards for electronic communications service operators. For IoT and connected wireless devices imported from China, the relevant considerations include: (1) assessment of whether the device or its associated cloud backend constitutes or serves an information system subject to Mongolian cybersecurity requirements; (2) data handling obligations if the device processes personal data of Mongolian users, including whether such data may be transferred to servers outside Mongolia; (3) compliance with any product-level cybersecurity requirements CITA may specify as part of the type approval process for connected telecommunications devices. Mongolia does not yet have a formal mandatory product-level cybersecurity certification scheme for consumer IoT equivalent to the EU Cyber Resilience Act, but the 2021 law establishes enforcement authority that could be applied to connected device operators and importers. Chinese CCC and SRRC approvals do not address Mongolian cybersecurity obligations.
summary.zh: 蒙古国于2021年颁布《信息与网络安全法》，为适用于信息系统、关键信息基础设施及电子通信服务和联网设备运营商的网络安全义务建立了国家框架。该法律由CITA（通信和信息技术局）和数字发展与通信部（原数字发展、创新与航天工业部）共同管理。2021年法律下的主要义务包括：影响信息系统的网络安全事件通知要求；关键信息基础设施运营商的安全要求；电子通信服务运营商的基础网络安全标准。对于从中国进口的物联网和联网无线设备，相关注意事项包括：(1) 评估设备或其关联云端后台是否构成或服务于蒙古国网络安全要求适用的信息系统；(2) 若设备处理蒙古国用户个人数据，则须履行数据处理义务，包括此类数据是否可传输至蒙古国境外服务器；(3) 遵守CITA在联网电信设备型号核准程序中可能规定的产品级网络安全要求。蒙古国尚未建立类似欧盟《网络弹性法案》的消费类物联网强制性产品级网络安全认证制度，但2021年法律确立了可适用于联网设备运营商和进口商的执法权威。中国CCC和SRRC核准不涉及蒙古国网络安全义务。
summary.zht: 蒙古國於2021年頒布《信息與網路安全法》，為適用於信息系統、關鍵信息基礎設施及電子通信服務和聯網設備運營商的網路安全義務建立了國家框架。該法律由CITA（通信和信息技術局）和數字發展與通信部（原數字發展、創新與航天工業部）共同管理。2021年法律下的主要義務包括：影響信息系統的網路安全事件通知要求；關鍵信息基礎設施運營商的安全要求；電子通信服務運營商的基礎網路安全標準。對於從中國進口的物聯網和聯網無線設備，相關注意事項包括：(1) 評估設備或其關聯雲端後台是否構成或服務於蒙古國網路安全要求適用的信息系統；(2) 若設備處理蒙古國用戶個人數據，則須履行數據處理義務，包括此類數據是否可傳輸至蒙古國境外服務器；(3) 遵守CITA在聯網電信設備型號核准程序中可能規定的產品級網路安全要求。蒙古國尚未建立類似歐盟《網路韌性法案》的消費類物聯網強制性產品級網路安全認證制度，但2021年法律確立了可適用於聯網設備運營商和進口商的執法權威。中國CCC和SRRC核准不涉及蒙古國網路安全義務。

standards_or_laws: Law of Mongolia on Information and Cybersecurity (2021) — establishes national cybersecurity obligations for information system operators, critical information infrastructure, and electronic communications service providers
CITA (Communications and Information Technology Authority of Mongolia) — administers cybersecurity obligations for telecommunications and ICT sectors under the 2021 law
Ministry of Digital Development and Communications of Mongolia — national policy authority for digital development, cybersecurity, and data governance

summary.en: China has its own comprehensive cybersecurity and data governance regime established under the Cybersecurity Law of the PRC (2017), the Data Security Law (2021), and the Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL, 2021). China also imposes data localization requirements for certain categories of data and mandates security assessments for cross-border data transfers approved by the Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC). For IoT and connected devices, MIIT and relevant sector regulators may impose network security and data-handling requirements as part of the NAL or network access process. Chinese domestic cybersecurity compliance under these laws does not satisfy Mongolia's obligations under the Information and Cybersecurity Law 2021 — they are entirely separate national frameworks with different enforcement authorities, scope, and obligations. Manufacturers exporting connected devices from China must separately assess Mongolian cybersecurity and data obligations for their device's data flows and backend infrastructure.
summary.zh: 中国依据《中华人民共和国网络安全法》（2017年）、《数据安全法》（2021年）和《个人信息保护法》（2021年）建立了完整的网络安全和数据治理制度。中国对某些类别数据实施数据本地化要求，并规定经网络安全审查通过的跨境数据传输须向国家互联网信息办公室（CAC）申报安全评估。对于物联网和联网设备，工业和信息化部及相关行业监管机构可能将网络安全和数据处理要求纳入NAL或网络接入程序。依据上述法律进行的中国国内网络安全合规不满足蒙古国2021年《信息与网络安全法》下的义务——两者属于完全独立的国家框架，执法主体、适用范围和义务各不相同。从中国出口联网设备的制造商须单独评估其设备数据流量和后台基础设施对应的蒙古国网络安全和数据义务。
summary.zht: 中國依據《中華人民共和國網路安全法》（2017年）、《數據安全法》（2021年）和《個人信息保護法》（2021年）建立了完整的網路安全和數據治理制度。中國對某些類別數據實施數據本地化要求，並規定經網路安全審查通過的跨境數據傳輸須向國家互聯網信息辦公室（CAC）申報安全評估。對於物聯網和聯網設備，工業和信息化部及相關行業監管機構可能將網路安全和數據處理要求納入NAL或網路接入程序。依據上述法律進行的中國國內網路安全合規不滿足蒙古國2021年《信息與網路安全法》下的義務——兩者屬於完全獨立的國家框架，執法主體、適用範圍和義務各不相同。從中國出口聯網設備的製造商須單獨評估其設備數據流量和後台基礎設施對應的蒙古國網路安全和數據義務。

standards_or_laws: Cybersecurity Law of the PRC (2017) — mandatory network security requirements for network operators and critical information infrastructure in China
Personal Information Protection Law of the PRC (PIPL, 2021) — personal data processing and cross-border transfer requirements in China
Data Security Law of the PRC (2021) — data classification, security, and cross-border data transfer restrictions in China

gap.en: Chinese domestic cybersecurity compliance (Cybersecurity Law, PIPL, Data Security Law) does not substitute for obligations under Mongolia's Information and Cybersecurity Law 2021. Manufacturers and importers of connected wireless and IoT devices must assess whether their device or associated cloud backend constitutes an information system or electronic communications service subject to Mongolian cybersecurity requirements. If the device processes personal data of Mongolian users, data transfer to servers outside Mongolia (including China) must be assessed against Mongolian data governance obligations. CITA may impose product-level cybersecurity requirements as part of the type approval process for connected telecommunications equipment — applicants should confirm current requirements directly with CITA. Mongolia currently has no formal mandatory consumer IoT product cybersecurity certification scheme equivalent to the EU Cyber Resilience Act, but the 2021 law provides enforcement authority that could be applied to importers and operators of non-compliant connected devices. Cross-border trade through the Erenhot/Zamiin-Uud land border with China is significant, and MCGA customs screening may include cybersecurity documentation review for telecommunications and radio equipment.
gap.zh: 中国国内网络安全合规（《网络安全法》、《个人信息保护法》、《数据安全法》）不可替代蒙古国2021年《信息与网络安全法》下的义务。联网无线及物联网设备的制造商和进口商须评估其设备或关联云端后台是否构成受蒙古国网络安全要求约束的信息系统或电子通信服务。若设备处理蒙古国用户个人数据，须依据蒙古国数据治理义务评估向蒙古国境外（包括中国）服务器传输数据的合规性。CITA可能在联网电信设备型号核准程序中提出产品级网络安全要求——申请人应直接向CITA确认当前要求。蒙古国目前尚无类似欧盟《网络弹性法案》的消费类物联网强制性产品网络安全认证制度，但2021年法律赋予的执法权可适用于不合规联网设备的进口商和运营商。通过二连浩特/扎门乌德中蒙陆路口岸的跨境贸易规模较大，蒙古国海关总署（MCGA）海关审查可能包括对电信和无线电设备网络安全文件的核查。
gap.zht: 中國國內網路安全合規（《網路安全法》、《個人信息保護法》、《數據安全法》）不可替代蒙古國2021年《信息與網路安全法》下的義務。聯網無線及物聯網設備的製造商和進口商須評估其設備或關聯雲端後台是否構成受蒙古國網路安全要求約束的信息系統或電子通信服務。若設備處理蒙古國用戶個人數據，須依據蒙古國數據治理義務評估向蒙古國境外（包括中國）服務器傳輸數據的合規性。CITA可能在聯網電信設備型號核准程序中提出產品級網路安全要求——申請人應直接向CITA確認當前要求。蒙古國目前尚無類似歐盟《網路韌性法案》的消費類物聯網強制性產品網路安全認證制度，但2021年法律賦予的執法權可適用於不合規聯網設備的進口商和運營商。通過二連浩特/扎門烏德中蒙陸路口岸的跨境貿易規模較大，蒙古國海關總署（MCGA）海關審查可能包括對電信和無線電設備網路安全文件的核查。

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory (framework law) — Mongolia Information and Cybersecurity Law 2021 imposes obligations on information system operators and electronic communications service providers; no formal mandatory consumer IoT product cybersecurity certification scheme currently in place; CITA may impose device-level security requirements within the type approval process for connected devices
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory — Cybersecurity Law, PIPL, and Data Security Law impose cybersecurity, data localization, and security assessment requirements for data processed in China; does not extend to or substitute for Mongolian obligations

source.publisher: CITA — Communications and Information Technology Authority of Mongolia; Ministry of Digital Development and Communications of Mongolia

compliance_verdict.en: [INFORMATIONAL] Mongolia's Information and Cybersecurity Law 2021 imposes cybersecurity obligations on information system operators and electronic communications service providers, which may encompass connected IoT device importers and their cloud backends. Chinese domestic cybersecurity compliance does not satisfy Mongolian obligations. There is currently no formal mandatory consumer IoT product cybersecurity certification scheme in Mongolia, but the law provides enforcement authority applicable to importers of non-compliant connected devices. CITA should be consulted directly for any product-level cybersecurity requirements within the type approval process. Data transfers from Mongolian users to China-based servers should be assessed against Mongolian data governance requirements.
compliance_verdict.zh: [仅供参考] 蒙古国2021年《信息与网络安全法》对信息系统运营商和电子通信服务提供商（可能涵盖联网物联网设备进口商及其云端后台）施加网络安全义务。中国国内网络安全合规不满足蒙古国义务。蒙古国目前尚无针对消费类物联网产品的强制性网络安全认证制度，但该法律赋予的执法权可适用于不合规联网设备的进口商。应直接咨询CITA了解型号核准程序中的产品级网络安全要求。蒙古国用户数据向中国境内服务器传输须依据蒙古国数据治理要求进行评估。
compliance_verdict.zht: [僅供參考] 蒙古國2021年《信息與網路安全法》對信息系統運營商和電子通信服務提供商（可能涵蓋聯網物聯網設備進口商及其雲端後台）施加網路安全義務。中國國內網路安全合規不滿足蒙古國義務。蒙古國目前尚無針對消費類物聯網產品的強制性網路安全認證制度，但該法律賦予的執法權可適用於不合規聯網設備的進口商。應直接諮詢CITA了解型號核准程序中的產品級網路安全要求。蒙古國用戶數據向中國境內服務器傳輸須依據蒙古國數據治理要求進行評估。
